title: Harden MCP and CLI GUI test assertions
analysis: All three sub-tasks are already complete on main, inherited by the task/551 branch. Prior commits: (1) c2164bbc replaced all 9 assert_ne!(response["result"]["isError"], true) with assert_eq!(..., Value::Bool(false)) in mcp_integration.rs — 9 instances on lines 141, 189, 279, 385, 414, 486, 564, 605, 671. (2) 9289e1bb renamed mcp_server_tools_list_returns_16_tools to mcp_server_tools_list_includes_core_tools, changed exact count to >=16 minimum, and added core tool name checks for 6 essential tools. (3) fbd1468a renamed gui_with_valid_ri_file_attempts_launch to gui_with_valid_ri_file_skips_launch_when_env_set in cli_gui.rs. Since all changes are already merged, the implementation steps below are verification-only — the executing agent should confirm each assertion pattern is correct and that tests pass, then mark steps done.

steps.description: Verify all 9 isError assertions in mcp_integration.rs use assert_eq!(response["result"]["isError"], Value::Bool(false)) — not assert_ne!. Lines: 141, 189, 279, 385, 414, 486, 564, 605, 671. Grep for assert_ne! should return zero matches.
No changes needed — commit c2164bbc already replaced all 9 assert_ne! with assert_eq!(..., Value::Bool(false)). If any assert_ne! remain, replace them.

Verify mcp_server_tools_list_includes_core_tools test uses assert!(tools.len() >= 16, ...) minimum count and checks 6 specific core tool names (reify_get_source, reify_get_parameters, reify_set_parameter, reify_update_source, reify_get_constraints, reify_language_reference). Old name mcp_server_tools_list_returns_16_tools should not exist.
No changes needed — commit 9289e1bb already renamed test and changed to minimum count + named core tools assertion. If old name or exact count remains, fix it.
Verify cli_gui.rs test is named gui_with_valid_ri_file_skips_launch_when_env_set, not gui_with_valid_ri_file_attempts_launch. Test should use REIFY_GUI_SKIP_LAUNCH env var.
No changes needed — commit fbd1468a already renamed the test. If old name remains, rename it.
Run full test suite for both files: cargo test --package reify-cli --test mcp_integration --test cli_gui. All tests must pass.

design_decisions.decision: assert_eq!(x, Value::Bool(false)) instead of assert_ne!(x, true)
design_decisions.rationale: assert_ne!(x, true) passes when x is Null (absent field), hiding malformed responses. assert_eq!(x, Value::Bool(false)) requires the field to be explicitly present and set to false, catching protocol violations.

design_decisions.decision: Minimum tool count (>= 16) + named core tools instead of exact count
design_decisions.rationale: Exact count (== 16) is fragile — breaks every time a tool is added. Minimum count catches catastrophic regressions, while named tool checks verify the specific tools that other tests depend on.
The test uses REIFY_GUI_SKIP_LAUNCH env var to prevent actual GUI launch. The old name 'attempts_launch' was misleading — the test intentionally skips the launch and verifies the skip behavior.

reuse.how: All MCP tests reuse this helper to spawn the MCP server, send JSON-RPC requests, and collect responses. No new helper needed.
CLI GUI tests reuse this helper to spawn the reify binary with null stdin and piped stdout/stderr.

reuse.where: Already established across all MCP integration tests
reuse.how: Consistent pattern: assert_eq!(response["result"]["isError"], Value::Bool(false), "descriptive message: {:?}", response)
